wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the KAIROS MCP project overview section. Explain the project's purpose as a TypeScript service for storing and executing reusable protocol chains for AI agents. Document the core concepts of persistent memory, deterministic execution, and agent-facing design. Describe the three main execution phases: activate, forward, and reward. Include the project's architecture overview showing MCP endpoints, REST APIs, browser UI, and CLI components. Explain the relationship between Qdrant vector storage, Keycloak authentication, and the React web interface. Provide practical examples of how AI agents interact with the system through the MCP protocol. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include diagrams where appropriate to illustrate key concepts.

wiki_overview.content:
Based on my comprehensive analysis of the Kairos MCP project, I can now generate a detailed overview:

<blog>

# KAIROS MCP: AI Agent Workflow Automation Platform

## Project Introduction

KAIROS MCP (Model Context Protocol) is a sophisticated TypeScript service designed to provide persistent memory and deterministic workflow execution for AI agents and chat systems. The platform addresses a critical gap in AI agent development: the inability to maintain context, follow multi-step procedures reliably, and avoid repeating work across sessions.

### Core Goals and Objectives

- **Persistent Memory Management**: Store and retrieve protocol chains across agent sessions
- **Deterministic Execution Flow**: Enforce structured workflow execution through standardized tool contracts
- **Agent-Friendly Design**: Provide clear, recoverable interfaces optimized for programmatic consumption
- **Multi-Modal Integration**: Support various embedding providers (OpenAI, TEI, Ollama) and vector databases (Qdrant)

### Target Audience

- AI agent developers seeking reliable workflow automation
- Organizations implementing enterprise-grade AI solutions
- Developers building autonomous systems requiring persistent context
- Teams deploying AI-powered automation workflows

## Technical Architecture

The KAIROS MCP platform follows a microservice architecture with clear separation of concerns across multiple layers:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Client Applications] --> B[MCP Protocol Handler]
    B --> C[HTTP REST API Layer]
    C --> D[Business Logic Services]
    D --> E[Qdrant Vector Database]
    D --> F[Redis Cache/Store]
    D --> G[Keycloak Authentication]
    C --> H[React Web UI]
    C --> I[CLI Interface]
    
    J[Embedding Providers] --> D
    K[External Tools] --> D
    
    style A fill:#e1f5fe
    style D fill:#f3e5f5
    style E fill:#fff3e0
    style G fill:#e8f5e8
```

### Component Breakdown

**Core Services Layer**:
- **Memory Management**: Qdrant-backed vector storage with semantic search capabilities
- **Authentication**: Keycloak OIDC integration with configurable group-based access control
- **Tool Registry**: Dynamic MCP tool registration and validation system
- **Cache Layer**: Optional Redis-based caching for performance optimization

**Interface Layer**:
- **MCP Protocol**: Standardized Model Context Protocol for agent communication
- **REST API**: HTTP endpoints for programmatic access
- **Web UI**: React-based interface for protocol management and monitoring
- **CLI**: Command-line interface for automation and scripting

**Integration Layer**:
- **Embedding Providers**: Support for multiple embedding services (OpenAI, TEI, Ollama)
- **External Systems**: Integration points for various AI tools and workflows

### Design Patterns

The architecture employs several key design patterns:

- **Repository Pattern**: Abstracted data access through MemoryQdrantStore
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic tool registration and configuration
- **Observer Pattern**: Event-driven updates for cache invalidation
- **Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable embedding providers and authentication methods

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Application Bootstrap** (`src/index.ts`):
- Initializes Qdrant connectivity with health checking
- Configures embedding dimensions and memory stores
- Sets up metrics server and HTTP application server
- Handles graceful startup and error recovery

**Server Initialization** (`src/server.ts`):
- Creates MCP server instance with tool registry
- Registers all available tools (activate, forward, train, etc.)
- Configures resource handlers and UI capabilities
- Establishes runtime configuration logging

**HTTP Server** (`src/http/http-server.ts`):
- Express-based HTTP server with middleware pipeline
- Authentication and authorization middleware
- Route handlers for all API endpoints
- Static file serving for web UI

### Core Modules

**Memory Management** (`src/services/memory/store.ts`):
- Qdrant client wrapper with collection alias resolution
- Adapter storage and retrieval operations
- Memory search with semantic similarity
- Artifact management and caching

**Tool Implementation** (`src/tools/activate.ts`):
- Semantic search engine for protocol matching
- Choice ranking with confidence scoring
- Linked artifact management and download capabilities
- Execution tracking and refinement support

**Configuration Management** (`src/config.ts`):
- Centralized environment variable parsing
- Authentication configuration (Keycloak OIDC)
- Embedding provider settings
- Performance tuning parameters

### Configuration Approach

**Environment-Based Configuration**:
- Comprehensive environment variable system
- Runtime configuration validation and masking
- Support for multiple embedding providers
- Flexible authentication schemes

**Deployment Configuration**:
- Docker Compose for local development
- Helm charts for Kubernetes deployments
- Multiple profile support (minimal, fullstack, production)

### External Dependencies

**Core Dependencies**:
- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk`: MCP protocol implementation
- `@qdrant/js-client-rest`: Qdrant vector database client
- `express`: HTTP server framework
- `react` & `react-dom`: Web UI framework
- `redis`: Optional caching layer

**Development Dependencies**:
- TypeScript for type safety
- Jest/Vitest for testing
- ESLint for code quality
- TailwindCSS for styling

### Integration Points

**Authentication Integration** (`src/http/http-auth-middleware.ts`):
- Keycloak OIDC integration
- JWT token validation
- Group-based access control
- Session management

**Embedding Provider Integration** (`src/services/embedding/`):
- OpenAI embeddings API
- TEI (Text Embedding Inference) support
- Custom embedding provider interface
- Performance monitoring and caching

**UI Integration** (`src/ui/`):
- React Router for navigation
- TanStack Query for data fetching
- Tiptap editor for protocol editing
- Theme system with dark/light modes

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[HTTP Server] --> B[Tool Registry]
    B --> C[Memory Store]
    C --> D[Qdrant Client]
    B --> E[Authentication]
    E --> F[Keycloak]
    B --> G[Embedding Service]
    G --> H[OpenAI/TEI]
    A --> I[React UI]
    A --> J[CLI]
    
    C --> K[Cache Layer]
    K --> L[Redis]
    
    style A fill:#4a6fa5
    style B fill:#ffb74d
    style C fill:#81c784
    style D fill:#64b5f6
    style E fill:#ff8a65
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Protocol Execution Engine**:
- Structured workflow execution (activate → forward → reward)
- Deterministic step-by-step processing
- Execution tracking and state management
- Error recovery and retry mechanisms

**Adaptive Learning System**:
- Semantic search with confidence scoring
- Protocol refinement and improvement
- Similar memory detection and reuse
- Performance optimization through caching

**Multi-Modal Interface Support**:
- Text-based protocol chains
- Structured data processing
- Artifact attachment and management
- Rich media support through linked artifacts

### Implementation Highlights

**Advanced Search Capabilities** (`src/tools/search.ts`):
- Semantic similarity search with multiple providers
- Confidence threshold filtering
- Group collapse for diverse results
- Overfetch and limit management

**Authentication and Authorization** (`src/http/http-auth-middleware.ts`):
- Flexible OIDC integration
- Group-based space isolation
- Token validation and refresh
- Session management and lifecycle

**Performance Optimization** (`src/services/redis-cache.ts`):
- Distributed caching for hot data
- Cache invalidation strategies
- Performance monitoring and metrics
- Connection pooling and management

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle
    Idle --> Searching: activate
    Searching --> Found: matches
    Searching --> Creating: no matches
    Found --> Executing: forward
    Executing --> Rewarding: completion
    Creating --> Training: save
    Training --> Executing: forward
    Rewarding --> Completed: success/failure
    Completed --> Idle: reset
    
    Executing --> Executing: continue_layers
    Executing --> Rewarding: final_layer
```

The KAIROS MCP platform represents a comprehensive solution for AI agent workflow automation, combining robust technical architecture with practical deployment considerations. Its modular design enables flexible deployment across different environments while maintaining consistent functionality and performance standards.
